scenario: 5 workspace switches via chrome-devtools MCP on dev:vite + dev:api at localhost:1420

topCallStacks.comment: useLayoutEffect calls node.offsetHeight per visible row -> O(n) forced layout flushes per render
scrollParent.scrollTop = scrollParent.scrollHeight reads scrollHeight after a DOM mutation, forcing layout

interpretationKeyFindings: The 250-300ms long frames during workspace switches are NOT JS-bound. They split roughly 50/50 between JS work (~150ms) and forcedStyleAndLayout (~120ms).

The single biggest forced reflow source is MeasuredConversationRow.reportHeight (286ms total across the 5 switches): every visible row reads node.offsetHeight inside useLayoutEffect, forcing an immediate layout flush that the next row also pays for.
ChatThread's snap-to-bottom useLayoutEffect contributes another 101ms of forced reflow by reading scrollHeight imperatively after each switch.

estimator:thread-heights is the only JS-side perf mark with a measurable cost (max 20ms, avg 6.4ms). All other perf marks (shareMessages, mergedMessages, visible-rows, rows) are sub-millisecond — they are NOT bottlenecks despite 21 prior optimization iterations targeting them.

Style recalc events of 114-124ms each affect 782-930 elements per recalc. With a 1423-element DOM, that means style invalidation is propagating to ~65% of the page on a single switch. This is likely caused by a className change near the panel root invalidating descendants — the cure is either CSS containment, content-visibility:auto on rows, or fewer global CSS variables in the cascade.

high_value_fixes: Batch / debounce MeasuredConversationRow height reads via a single ResizeObserver instead of per-row useLayoutEffect (eliminates the O(n) forced reflow chain)
Defer ChatThread's snap-to-bottom scrollTop write to a microtask after the next paint, OR use scroll-anchoring CSS (overflow-anchor) instead of imperative scroll
Add `content-visibility: auto` + `contain-intrinsic-size` to MeasuredConversationRow so off-screen rows skip layout/style entirely
Investigate which CSS class change is invalidating 930 elements on a single workspace switch — narrow the style scope or add `contain: style` on the panel root
low_value_fixes_to_skip: Any further optimization of shareMessages, mergedMessages, viewport:rows, viewport:visible-rows — they're all sub-millisecond and the surgical iterations of the past 21 rounds were targeting noise
Direct DOM updates for streaming markdown (A4 research) — Streamdown is the right choice, that path is already optimal
ContextMenu lazy mount and Lucide icon hoisting — sidebar memo bail-out works correctly during workspace switches (only 10 row renders across 5 switches)
medium_value_fixes: Web Worker for estimateThreadRowHeights (A2) — saves up to 20ms per switch on cold render but won't move the long-frame distribution by much

A1' deferred hydration: render only the last N messages on initial mount, hydrate the rest after a microtask. Cuts the React reconciliation cost (~150ms JS) by reducing the number of MemoConversationMessage commits per frame
